response: {"purpose":"Figure 4a–d demonstrates that the eCLIP paired size-matched input (SMInput) normalization strategy enables accurate, RNA-centric identification of RNA-binding protein (RBP) occupancy on highly abundant noncoding RNAs, such as the lincRNA MALAT1, which are difficult to assess with traditional CLIP methods due to their high background read density. Panels (a) and (b) establish that the majority of CLIPper-identified clusters across 102 eCLIP experiments are genuinely enriched over SMInput rather than depleted, validating the specificity of the approach across diverse RBPs and genomic regions. Panels (c) and (d) then illustrate the biological utility of this normalization by revealing that different RBPs exhibit strikingly distinct and reproducible binding patterns along MALAT1, patterns that would be obscured without proper input correction. This matters because it shows eCLIP can reliably distinguish true protein–RNA interactions from spurious signal arising from transcript abundance, enabling systematic and quantitative profiling of RBP binding to noncoding RNA loci.","title":"eCLIP resolves protein binding to abundant noncoding RNAs over background"}
